title: In the Porch of Solomon
subtitle: At the Feast of Dedication, the Jews surround Jesus in the temple, demanding plainly: are you the Christ?

stillness: It was winter, and the feast of dedication had come.
Be still, and let the temple within you be renewed.
Compose your heart in silence before the Word of God.
gospel: Now the feast of the dedication was kept at Jerusalem,
And Jesus walked in the temple, in Solomon's porch.
The Jews therefore came round about him,
How long dost thou hold our soul in suspense?
If thou be the Christ, tell us plainly.
Jesus answered them: I speak to you, and you believe not.
The works that I do in the name of my Father,
because you are not of my sheep.
and I know them, and they follow me.
And I give them life everlasting;
and they shall not perish for ever,
and no man shall pluck them out of my hand.
I and the Father are one.
behold: It is winter, and the feast of the temple's dedication has come.
See Jesus walking in the temple, in the porch of Solomon,
the place where Solomon himself once stood to pray,
where the people stood in the porches and courts surrounding the temple,
for none might enter the temple itself, save the ministers alone.
Behold the Jews crowding round about him,
pressing close, with intent to encircle and entrap him,
that they might the more quickly catch him in his words.
Hear their flattering speech: How long dost thou hold our soul in suspense?
They did not desire the truth, but were preparing a calumny.
Hear, then, how the Lord tempers his answer,
that no place be left them for their slander:
The works that I do, these give testimony of me;
for they cannot be done save by God,
and so it plainly appears that I have come from God.
consider: They speak with flattering lips, feigning to long for the truth,
yet they held the very contrary hidden in their heart.
They did not desire the truth, but were preparing a calumny.
And because they spoke with guile, the Lord tempered his answer.
He did not say plainly, I am the Christ, as they demanded,
but said something of equal force, or greater, which yet did not satisfy them.
Consider the cause of their unbelief, which he lays bare:
You believe not, because you are not of my sheep,
not innocent and humble, but proud and malicious,
and therefore blinded by the judgment of God.
But my sheep, that is, the humble, hear my voice,
believing in heart and obeying in work; and I know them.
And I give them life everlasting, here in the food of my Body and Blood,
and hereafter in the sweetness of fruition;
and no man shall pluck them out of my hand by violence,
neither dragging them here to guilt, nor hereafter to punishment.
For that which the Father gave me is greater than all,
and exceeds all things without end: I and the Father are one.
prayer: Take care, then, that you also,
while this present life is shaken by the storms of iniquity,
may celebrate the spiritual dedication of your own temple,
and setting ascensions in your heart.
Then Jesus will be present in the porch of Solomon,
granting you a state of peace under his own governing.
For in the world to come,
no one will be able to accomplish the solemnities of renewal.

latinJewel.latin: Oves meæ vocem meam audiunt, credendo corde, et obediendo opere; et ego cognosco eas.
latinJewel.english: My sheep hear my voice, believing in heart and obeying in work; and I know them.
